release.title: R3: Turn task and release reports into trustworthy publish artifacts
release.summary: Ship the single high-priority goal `Turn task and release reports into trustworthy publish artifacts` as a standalone bundled release.

brief.objective: Make published task and release reports trustworthy by construction.
brief.target_user_value: Operators can trust that anything published to docs/reports and docs/releases is concrete, not template filler.
brief.why_now: The loop now produces better telemetry and state history, so the next trust gap is report content quality.
brief.packaging_rationale: Placeholder blocking and better release aggregation belong together because both determine whether the top-level release artifact is believable.
scope_in: Detect placeholder content in task and release reports
Filter placeholder lines out of release-report aggregation
release_acceptance: Task publish fails if the report still contains placeholder guidance text
Release publish fails if the release report or bundled task reports still contain placeholders
Release aggregation keeps real delivered and observation lines while dropping template filler
brief.launch_story: This release turns the reporting layer into a real trust boundary: if a report is published, it contains real content.
deferred_items: Strengthen PM release planning with archetypes and better packaging signals
Add operator-facing status and doctor tooling for loop recovery

goal_titles: Turn task and release reports into trustworthy publish artifacts

task_iterations.goal: Turn task and release reports into trustworthy publish artifacts

research_findings: Existing release reports can still preserve template guidance text when task reports or release-closeout inputs are incomplete.
A publish gate is the right enforcement point because it protects Git history without making report drafting unusable.
Release aggregation also needs to skip placeholder lines, otherwise reports can look populated while still containing empty template guidance.

research_gate.summary: Report trust now needs hard gates so published artifacts cannot contain template filler.

product_council.summary: Approve: published reports are user-facing artifacts, so placeholder content is a direct trust failure.

architecture_council.summary: Approve: a shared placeholder detector in common.py creates one consistent gate for task and release publication.

operator_council.summary: Approve: operators should fail fast with a clear explanation instead of noticing bad release notes after publish.

delivery_secretary.summary: Detect placeholder content centrally, enforce it in publish gates, and strip placeholder lines out of release aggregation.
delivery_secretary.next_action: Run validation and then publish the report hardening release.

audit_secretary.summary: Observed release reports prove that report-level placeholder text can currently leak into durable artifacts.
audit_secretary.decision_record: Proceed with placeholder gating plus aggregation cleanup as one bundled release.

scope_decision.selected_goal: Turn task and release reports into trustworthy publish artifacts
scope_decision.why_selected: The release experience still breaks if the top-level artifact reads like a template instead of a real release note.
scope_in: Detect known placeholder guidance text in reports
Block task and release publish when placeholders remain
Ignore placeholder lines when aggregating release delivery and output sections
scope_out: Do not change release planning prompts yet
Do not add status or doctor commands yet
stop_conditions: Stop if placeholder gating breaks seeded validator flows or blocks concrete reports

critique: This release protects a user-visible quality boundary without widening scope into planning or recovery UX.
The gate is deterministic because it relies on explicit placeholder markers instead of subjective heuristics.

review_state.goal_title: Turn task and release reports into trustworthy publish artifacts
